mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
mcqs.answer: A. A body at rest will stay at rest unless acted upon by an external force.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of inertia?
mcqs.answer: A. A person sitting in a moving bus feels pushed back when the bus suddenly stops.

mcqs.question: Inertia depends on which property of an object?
If a hockey puck slides on ice, it will continue to slide until:
An object will remain in motion unless:
mcqs.answer: B. It is acted upon by an unbalanced force.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: According to Newton's First Law, an object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by a __________.
The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion is called __________.
An object's mass is a measure of its __________.
A __________ force is required to change the motion of an object.
Newton's First Law is also known as the law of __________.

short_questions.question: What is inertia?
short_questions.answer: Inertia is the property of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.

short_questions.question: Give an example of Newton's First Law in everyday life.
short_questions.answer: A book on a table remains at rest until someone picks it up.

short_questions.question: How does mass affect inertia?
short_questions.answer: Greater mass results in greater inertia, making it harder to change its motion.

short_questions.question: What happens to a ball rolling on a surface if no external forces act on it?
short_questions.answer: It will continue rolling indefinitely at a constant speed.

short_questions.question: Why is it important to wear seat belts in a car?
short_questions.answer: To counteract inertia; during sudden stops, the body tends to continue moving forward.
